feed.label: Product System
feed.title: Expanding the School Management System
feed.description: Continuing work on the multi-tenant school platform with stronger module structure, workflow clarity, and production-focused backend decisions.
points: Refining admin, staff, and student workflows
Improving modular API structure for long-term maintainability
Thinking around scale, auth, and production-readiness instead of demo code

feed.label: AI Engineering
feed.title: Learning LLM Integration & AI System Patterns
feed.description: Learning LLM APIs, RAG concepts, AI agent architecture, and understanding how intelligent systems integrate with backend engineering.
points: Exploring RAG pipeline design for grounded document Q&A
Studying LLM integration patterns and prompt engineering
Experimenting with Python to understand AI/ML engineering concepts

feed.label: Brand + Frontend
feed.title: Turning the Portfolio into a Structured Product
feed.description: Building the portfolio like an actual system — not a random personal site — with reusable sections, better positioning, and stronger frontend structure.
points: Creating consistent page structure across sections
Improving frontend quality without adding fluff
Positioning work around engineering depth instead of generic self-promotion

feed.label: Engineering Thinking
feed.title: Studying Production-Grade System Design
feed.description: Going deeper into scalability, caching, architecture trade-offs, API design, horizontal scaling, and failure-aware backend engineering.
points: Learning when to use and when not to use caching
Thinking through scaling models instead of copying buzzwords
Focusing on real trade-offs in backend architecture

feed.label: Public Work
feed.title: Contributing Through Public Code and Engineering Learning
feed.description: Using GitHub and open source as proof of actual work, experimentation, and exposure to better engineering practices.
points: Learning from real repositories instead of isolated tutorial projects
Improving code quality by reading stronger engineering patterns
Treating open source as a real training ground

feed.label: Next Frontier
feed.title: Learning More About AI, ML, and Intelligent Systems
feed.description: Exploring AI and ML concepts to understand how modern software systems are shifting beyond traditional CRUD applications.
points: Trying to understand AI systems from an engineering angle
Connecting backend/system thinking with modern AI product directions
Learning the fundamentals instead of pretending to be an expert already

snapshots.name: Building Systems
snapshots.description: Backend-heavy products, multi-tenant architecture, and maintainable full-stack systems.

snapshots.name: Learning in Public
snapshots.description: AI/ML engineering, LLM integration, RAG pipelines, system design, and stronger production engineering habits.

snapshots.name: Open Source Direction
snapshots.description: Using GitHub work and repository exploration as a visible trail of engineering growth.

timeline.title: Building deeper engineering foundations
timeline.detail: Focusing on system design, backend architecture, full-stack product quality, and more serious project depth.

timeline.title: B.Tech in Computer Science
timeline.detail: Continuing formal education while building practical projects and sharpening engineering skills outside the classroom.

timeline.title: Open source and public learning
timeline.detail: Using GitHub, public repos, and engineering communities to improve through real code exposure.

timeline.title: AI/ML systems exploration
timeline.detail: Pushing toward AI engineering, LLM systems, RAG pipelines, and stronger understanding of ML system thinking.
